title: Intolerance

evidence.note: Published 1916; the 95-year US copyright term has expired for works published through 1930, so the film print is public domain in the US by term expiry.

diy_links.label: Catalog of Copyright Entries (archive.org) — browse the Motion Pictures renewal-year volume by hand
US Copyright Office public records (1978+ renewals/assignments)

editorial: Intolerance is a 1916 American silent epic directed by D. W. Griffith, interweaving four stories across history — Babylonian, Judean, French Renaissance and modern American — on a then-unprecedented scale, produced independently by Griffith's Wark Producing Corporation. Published in 1916, the film print's US copyright term has expired, placing the photoplay in the public domain by term expiry. Later restorations may carry their own separate rights.
